title: crucianella glauca subsp. gilanica
intro: Crucianella glauca subsp. gilanica, a sub-species of the Crucianella glauca plant, is a type of flowering plant in the broomrape family (Orobanchaceae), native to Iran, particularly in the Gilan Province. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which contribute to its health benefits. The health benefits of Crucianella glauca subsp. gilanica include reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and possessing antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
